title: EventFacility
description: Pivot row attaching a Facility to an Event. An Event may attach 0..N facilities (0 for virtual awards / camp-openings held outdoors; N for multi-facility tournaments). Carries `tenant_id` + `event_id` + `facility_id`. Cascades organization + region + branch through facility_id. NEVER carries `application_id` / `organization_id` / `region_id` / `branch_id` / `scope_node_id`. Exactly one `is_primary=true` per event (partial unique index).

event_id.description: CASCADE — the pivot's lifetime is bounded by the event.

facility_id.description: RESTRICT — a facility with active event attachments cannot be retired without first detaching the events (matches the facility module's cascade rules).

is_primary.description: At most one primary facility per event. The primary is what the event card + notifications reference as 'the venue'.

purpose.description: Enum: competition / warm_up / registration / spectator / vip. Describes what the facility is used FOR at this event.

indexes.name: event_facilities_pair_unique
indexes.description: A facility attaches to an event once (multi-attachment for different purposes is an anti-pattern; use one row + set purpose to the most-important use).

indexes.name: event_facilities_primary_unique
indexes.predicate: is_primary = true AND deleted_at IS NULL
indexes.description: At most one primary per event.

x-invariants: facility.branch_id resolves to a branch in the event's organization (when event.branch_id IS NOT NULL, they should match; when event.branch_id IS NULL the facility's branch may be any within event.organization_id)
event_id + facility_id IMMUTABLE post-create — to move a facility, delete + recreate
COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE is_primary=true AND deleted_at IS NULL) <= 1 per event_id
event.status NOT IN ('completed', 'cancelled', 'archived') at create time — cannot attach a facility to a finished event
